attack_chain.description: Attacker discovered that PREMINT's S3 bucket (s3-redwood-labs.premint.xyz) was publicly misconfigured, allowing unauthenticated list, read, and write access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: we suspect that there was a misconfiguration in S3, leading to unauthorized access to the Bucket, allowing attackers to list, read, or write to the S3 bucket at will

attack_chain.description: Attacker registered a typosquatting domain (s3-redwood-labs-premint-xyz.com) mimicking the legitimate S3 subdomain to host the secondary malicious payload.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: attack script file hosted on a fake domain owned by the hackers (s3-redwood-labs-premint-xyz.com)

attack_chain.description: Attacker prepared and hosted a malicious JavaScript payload (cdn.min.js) on their attacker-controlled domain, designed to replace wallet signature requests with setApprovalForAll NFT authorization transactions.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: cdn.min.js?v=1658050292559. This script contained interactions to deceive users into granting authorization

attack_chain.description: Attacker exploited the publicly writable S3 bucket misconfiguration to gain write access to PREMINT's hosted static assets without authentication.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: misconfiguration in S3, leading to unauthorized access to the Bucket, allowing attackers to list, read, or write to the S3 bucket at will, thus tampering with the JS resource files

attack_chain.description: Attacker injected a malicious loader snippet into the legitimate boomerang.min.js file hosted in the S3 bucket, which dynamically fetches and executes the attacker's secondary script from the typosquatted domain.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: This file was injected by hackers with a script that loads another attack script file hosted on a fake domain owned by the hackers

attack_chain.description: When users visited the legitimate premint.xyz website, their browsers loaded the tampered boomerang.min.js, which in turn fetched the attack script from the attacker-controlled C2 domain (s3-redwood-labs-premint-xyz.com/cdn.min.js).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the website loads the following JS resource file https://s3-redwood-labs.premint.xyz/theme/js/boomerang.min.js ... loads another attack script file hosted on a fake domain

attack_chain.description: The attacker's injected script intercepted users' normal wallet-signing flow during PREMINT's 'Verify your wallet ownership' step, silently replacing the benign message signature request with a malicious setApprovalForAll on-chain transaction.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: this script was triggered, replacing the original verification signature with a transaction that authorized the attacker to transfer users' high-value NFTs

attack_chain.description: Once users unknowingly signed the setApprovalForAll transaction, the attacker used the obtained NFT transfer authorization to steal high-value NFTs from victims' wallets.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: After obtaining the authorization, the attacker stole users' NFTs and other assets

initial_access_vector: S3 bucket global write misconfiguration allowed unauthenticated attacker to overwrite PREMINT's publicly hosted JavaScript asset (boomerang.min.js)
persistence_mechanism: Malicious JS loader remained injected in the S3-hosted boomerang.min.js file for at least 6 hours after discovery, continuing to serve the tampered file to all site visitors
privilege_escalation: None required — S3 bucket misconfiguration granted public write access without authentication
